hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts while disrupting off-target interactions. Mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (e.g., L, I, V) will improve steric complementarity in SSTR2-specific TM6 subpockets and reduce off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve SSTR2 selectivity beyond native SST-14. A focused mutagenesis approach at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to disrupt off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target contacts are likely to occur (e.g., SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved pocket residues).
Introduce mutations at key off-target interaction sites to reduce SSTR3/SSTR5 binding while preserving SSTR2 selectivity.
